Project.NAME: ai-steward-wiki
Project.keywords: telegram, claude-code, codex, provider-failover, wiki, multi-user, classifier, scheduler, sqlite, alembic, aiogram, async
Project.annotation: Isolated multi-user Telegram WIKI assistant with Claude as primary and subscription-backed Codex failover. The graph covers provider policy and adapters, foundation, storage, auth, scheduler, classifiers, WIKI runtime, inbox, ops, Telegram I/O, media, pipelines, and runtime wiring.

M-LLM-FAILOVER.NAME: LLM provider failover policy
M-LLM-FAILOVER.purpose: Select Claude, Codex, or one Claude probe and permit fallback only for typed subscription limits and proven-safe replay.

type-ProviderState.PURPOSE: Fixed enum with claude, codex, and probe values.

type-EvidenceKind.PURPOSE: Fixed read-only, mutation, delivered, and unknown effect categories.

type-ProviderLimitError.PURPOSE: Typed subscription-limit error with provider and optional reset time.

type-AttemptEvidence.PURPOSE: Fail-closed side-effect evidence for replay decisions.

type-ReplayBlockedError.PURPOSE: Unsafe same-operation replay rejection carrying observed evidence.

type-ProvidersUnavailableError.PURPOSE: Typed primary and fallback failure pair.

type-FailoverEvent.PURPOSE: Sanitized provider selection, transition, failure, and recovery event.

type-FailoverMetrics.PURPOSE: Process-local primary, fallback, dual-failure, replay, and recovery counters.

type-FailoverPolicy.PURPOSE: Atomic process-local selection, transition, cooldown, probe, and replay orchestration.

M-LLM-CODEX.NAME: Codex CLI subscription adapter
M-LLM-CODEX.purpose: Execute Codex CLI with ChatGPT subscription authentication under explicit model, sandbox, working-directory, and output contracts.

type-CodexCliAdapter.PURPOSE: Structured, agent, text, and readiness entry points.

type-CodexReadiness.PURPOSE: Binary, version, authentication, and non-interactive readiness result.

type-CodexRequest.PURPOSE: Validated prompt, model, reasoning, capability, timing, and path request without credentials.

type-CodexRunKind.PURPOSE: Fixed structured, text, agent-read, agent-write, and web capability profiles.

type-CodexEvent.PURPOSE: Provider-neutral assistant, tool, final, or raw event without WIKI-layer dependency.

type-CodexUnavailableError.PURPOSE: Sanitized binary, process, timeout, auth, version, or readiness failure.

type-CodexOutputError.PURPOSE: Malformed structured, text, or JSONL output failure.

fn-normalize_codex_event.PURPOSE: Map Codex JSONL into provider-neutral events and mutation evidence.

M-FOUNDATION-SETTINGS.NAME: Settings
M-FOUNDATION-SETTINGS.purpose: Pydantic-settings BaseSettings (frozen) loaded from .env with AISW_ prefix.

type-Settings.PURPOSE: Frozen BaseSettings: log_level, workspace_root, *_db_url, default_user_tz (fallback IANA TZ for NL time, default 'Europe/Moscow', aisw-kcz). No claude_config_dir setting — the bot uses the run user's default ~/.claude via claude_cli.common.default_claude_config_dir (ADR-009 final).

type-LogLevel.PURPOSE: Literal alias for accepted log levels

type-ReasoningEffort.PURPOSE: Closed low, medium, or high Codex reasoning levels

type-CodexSettings.PURPOSE: Provider enablement, positive cooldown, binary, pinned version, CODEX_HOME, models, and reasoning without API-key billing

M-FOUNDATION-LOGGING.NAME: Logging
M-FOUNDATION-LOGGING.purpose: structlog JSON-lines logging with correlation_id contextvar propagation.

fn-configure_logging.PURPOSE: One-shot structlog setup, JSON renderer, ISO UTC timestamps

fn-bind_correlation_id.PURPOSE: Set the correlation_id contextvar; returns Token

fn-reset_correlation_id.PURPOSE: Reset via Token returned from bind_correlation_id

M-AUTH-USERS.NAME: Auth / Users
M-AUTH-USERS.purpose: users.toml SSoT allowlist with SIGHUP+watchdog hot-reload and sessions.db sync (D-031, D-042).

type-UserRecord.PURPOSE: Frozen Pydantic model for one [[users]] entry

type-AllowlistReloader.PURPOSE: Guarded async reload coroutine; sha256 short-circuit

fn-replace_global.PURPOSE: Atomic swap of module-level allowlist cache

fn-sync_to_sessions_db.PURPOSE: Upsert + soft-disable for users absent from config

M-STORAGE.NAME: Storage barrel
M-STORAGE.purpose: Storage package root; re-exports apply_sqlite_pragmas plus SQLite PRAGMA listener.

fn-apply_sqlite_pragmas.PURPOSE: Attach connect-listener that sets WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000

M-STORAGE-JOBS.NAME: Storage / jobs.db
M-STORAGE-JOBS.purpose: Async engine + ORM models + Pydantic discriminated union for jobs.db (D-002, D-006, D-014, D-019).

type-Job.PURPOSE: Row in jobs.jobs (flat columns + JSON payload)

type-DigestPayload.PURPOSE: kind='digest' payload: wiki_scope:'all'|list[str] (non-empty; aisw-269 — no jobs.db migration), recurrence:Recurrence, window_hours:int=24 (1..168), prompt_hint:str|None (aisw-oqq)

M-STORAGE-AUDIT.NAME: Storage / audit.db
M-STORAGE-AUDIT.purpose: Append-only audit + dedup + observability for audit.db (D-006 amended 2026-05-10).

type-ChatTurn.PURPOSE: Frozen view of one recent chat_log row (direction/text/ts) (D-033, aisw-kml)

fn-ChatLogWriter.PURPOSE: audit.db adapter: write_in/write_out + read_recent_window (D-033 last-20/24h conversation buffer fed to Stage-0/Stage-1a; aisw-kml)

fn-redact_chat_text.PURPOSE: drop D-033 denylist secrets (sk-ant-/Bearer/password=) before persisting plaintext (aisw-kml)

M-STORAGE-SESSIONS.NAME: Storage / sessions.db
M-STORAGE-SESSIONS.purpose: Runtime TG state and hot-path caches for sessions.db (D-006, D-031, D-042, D-030, D-023; user_digest_prefs per-user digest section toggles — ADR-025/ADR-026, aisw-pv8).

type-InboxHintCache.PURPOSE: Per-user runtime catalog of '## Inbox hint'

type-UserDigestPrefs.PURPOSE: Per-owner digest section toggles (trackers/wiki on-off; FK users.user_id CASCADE; absent row ⇒ all on) — ADR-026, aisw-pv8

type-DigestPrefs.PURPOSE: Frozen view of one owner's digest toggles (+ disabled_keys); both True when no row / no user

type-TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS.PURPOSE: ('trackers','wiki') — the digest section keys an owner may toggle (subset of M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING EXPAND_SECTION_KEYS)

type-SECTION_DISPLAY_NAME.PURPOSE: toggleable section key → ru label with emoji (for /digest_sections buttons + the fire_digest_job directive)

fn-get_digest_prefs.PURPOSE: telegram_id → DigestPrefs (both True if no users row / no prefs row); read-only

fn-set_digest_section.PURPOSE: upsert one section's bool for telegram_id (creates the row with defaults if absent; bumps updated_at_utc); no-op + defaults if telegram_id has no users row; ValueError on unknown section

type-UserActiveWiki.PURPOSE: Per-user sticky last-active-<Domain>-WIKI pointer (one row per telegram_id; alembic sessions 0004) (aisw-0ym)

fn-ActiveWikiPointer.PURPOSE: sessions.db adapter: set_active (upsert on successful route/ingest) / get_active (24h TTL guard == chat_log D-033 window; stale ⇒ None) (aisw-0ym)

M-RUNTIME-WIRING.NAME: Runtime wiring / __main__
M-RUNTIME-WIRING.purpose: Process entrypoint (python -m ai_steward_wiki). Composes Settings, per-DB alembic migrations, engines, allowlist sync, APScheduler (incl. the daily media _staging sweep job), classifier+runner+output adapters, VoiceHandler/PhotoIngestor, aiogram Bot+Dispatcher; runs long-polling with graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown. The runner adapter promotes staged media into <wiki>/raw/media after a successful Stage-1 run (D-022). aisw-o6m (ADR-034): _WikiRunnerAdapter.run resolves group-2 (wiki_query) scope via wiki.scope.resolve_query_scope before assembling the prompt — a confident single-domain match swaps wiki_path/wiki_id to run scoped inside that WIKI, else the run stays cross-root with a wiki.scope.collect_layouts block injected into the scratch overlay; _DigestRunnerAdapter injects the same layouts block for non-primary WIKIs (extra_add_dirs) on full-digest runs (section-expand runs skip it). Both degrade to legacy cross-root behaviour on any resolver error (FR-6, wiki.run.scope.degraded).
M-RUNTIME-WIRING.depends: M-FOUNDATION-SETTINGS, M-FOUNDATION-LOGGING, M-AUTH-USERS, M-STORAGE-JOBS, M-STORAGE-AUDIT, M-STORAGE-SESSIONS, M-SCHEDULER, M-SCHEDULER-FIRING, M-CLASSIFIER-STAGE0, M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE, M-WIKI-RUNNER, M-WIKI-LIFECYCLE, M-WIKI-SCOPE, M-TG-TEXT, M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER, M-TG-VOICE, M-TG-PHOTO, M-INBOX, M-INBOX-ROUTER, M-INBOX-ROUTE, M-OPS-PII, M-LLM-FAILOVER, M-LLM-CODEX

fn-_sync_url_for_jobstore.PURPOSE: strip aiosqlite driver from URL for SQLAlchemyJobStore

fn-_ensure_data_dirs.PURPOSE: mkdir parents for sqlite file URLs

fn-_run_all_migrations.PURPOSE: alembic upgrade head per-DB via asyncio.to_thread

fn-_load_users_config.PURPOSE: load users.toml or return empty config when path missing

type-_RouterAdapter.PURPOSE: Inbox-WIKI Stage-1a router: ensure_inbox_wiki → stage raw payload → run_wiki_session in Inbox-WIKI/ → parse_router_reply; WikiRunnerError→RouterError (aisw-dsg)

fn-_render_raw_sidecar.PURPOSE: build (filename, content) for Inbox-WIKI/raw/<ts>_<source>.md — plain body for text, YAML front-matter + staged_path sidecar for media

type-_LibrarianAdapter.PURPOSE: Inbox-WIKI Stage-1b librarian: resolve_target_wiki → stage_raw_into_wiki → run_wiki_session in <Domain>-WIKI/ (wiki.md + domain overlay) → IngestOutcome; WikiRunnerError→run_failed (aisw-zd9)

type-_RecurrenceParserAdapter.PURPOSE: binds classifier.recurrence.parse_recurrence with the Stage-0 backend + prompts/recurrence.md Haiku-fallback prompt (None → escalate-on-miss); passed into DefaultPipeline (aisw-oqq)

fn-_resolve_owner_wikis.PURPOSE: list the owner's <Name>-WIKI/ dirs under the workspace root (minus Inbox-WIKI) for fire_digest_job's --add-dir set; bound into firing.set_digest_context (aisw-oqq)

type-_WikiRunnerAdapter.PURPOSE: Stage-1a/1b runner adapter; gained hint_catalog_resolver + owner_wikis_resolver ctor params (aisw-o6m, ADR-034) — .run() calls wiki.scope.resolve_query_scope for WIKI_QUERY only (WEB_TASK and other intents untouched); confident match swaps wiki_path/wiki_id to run scoped inside that WIKI (CLAUDE.md arrives via assemble_prompt, no layouts injection); ambiguous/unwired/resolver-error stays cross-root with wiki.scope.collect_layouts appended to the scratch overlay text; logs wiki.run.scope / wiki.run.scope.degraded

type-_DigestRunnerAdapter.PURPOSE: digest runner adapter; full-digest calls (section=None) append wiki.scope.collect_layouts over extra_add_dirs (non-primary WIKIs) to planner_context before user_input assembly (aisw-o6m, ADR-034) — the primary WIKI's CLAUDE.md still arrives via assemble_prompt; section-expand calls (/expand) skip layouts injection (scoped re-run, no cross-WIKI ambiguity); OSError degrades silently (wiki.run.scope.degraded)

M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING.NAME: Telegram handlers wiring
M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING.purpose: aiogram Router that adapts Telegram message/callback events to a MessagePipeline (+ the bot's slash commands): /digest_now, /expand <section> (aisw-269) and /digest_sections (aisw-pv8) — Command-filter handlers reaching scheduler.firing accessors; the digestsec: toggle callback; then text/voice/photo/audio/video_note/document and the confirm callback, composed into the Dispatcher. The text handler already excludes "/"-prefixed messages, so command order is irrelevant. audio and video_note route to on_voice (STT path); photo forwards message.caption; photos and image-documents are forwarded with media_paths.
M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING.depends: M-TG-TEXT, M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER, M-TG-VOICE, M-TG-PHOTO, M-SCHEDULER-FIRING, M-STORAGE-SESSIONS

fn-build_router.PURPOSE: factory wiring the slash commands (/digest_now, /expand, /digest_sections) + the digestsec: callback + the 7 message/callback handlers (text, voice, photo, audio, video_note, document, confirm-callback) to a MessagePipeline

fn-parse_digestsec_callback.PURPOSE: parse digestsec:<section>:<0|1> → (section, target_enabled) or None (the flag is the TARGET state — idempotent on a stale message) — aisw-pv8

type-DIGESTSEC_CALLBACK_PREFIX.PURPOSE: 'digestsec:' callback_data prefix for the /digest_sections toggle buttons (aisw-pv8)

type-EXPAND_SECTION_KEYS.PURPOSE: ('today','meds','trackers','wiki') — the /expand <section> keys, mirroring the D-024 digest headers (aisw-269)

M-CLAUDE-CLI-COMMON.NAME: ClaudeCliCommon
M-CLAUDE-CLI-COMMON.purpose: Pure-function primitives shared by Stage-0 (classifier) and Stage-1 (wiki) Claude CLI backends.

fn-default_claude_config_dir.PURPOSE: ~/.claude — the run user's default Claude subscription-auth dir (ADR-009 final; no dedicated dir, no AISW_CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR)

fn-build_env.PURPOSE: restricted env dict (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR + minimal PATH) for CLI subprocess

fn-neutral_cwd.PURPOSE: working directory that does NOT auto-discover project CLAUDE.md

fn-system_prompt_argv.PURPOSE: inline --system-prompt flag fragment replacing the default Claude Code system prompt

fn-truncate_stderr.PURPOSE: UTF-8 decode + length cap for error log lines

type-ClaudeSubscriptionLimit.PURPOSE: Confirmed structured Claude HTTP 429 with optional reset time

fn-parse_claude_subscription_limit.PURPOSE: Recognize confirmed limit envelopes without stderr heuristics

M-ONBOARD-ADMIN.NAME: Onboarding / Admin
M-ONBOARD-ADMIN.purpose: Unknown-telegram_id onboarding (pending_users CRUD + intro template) and admin surface — approve/reject pending users, elevation, shadow channel (D-028, D-030, D-031, D-032, D-042).

fn-start_unknown_user.PURPOSE: idempotent upsert returning the refreshed pending row

fn-purge_expired_pending.PURPOSE: delete pending rows past TTL; returns deleted count

type-ElevationToken.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass returned by elevate; default 30min window

M-SCHEDULER.NAME: Scheduler core
M-SCHEDULER.purpose: APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler factory (D-003), 5-lane priority queue, 3-tier WIKI lock manager, failure taxonomy + 3-strikes auto-disable, DLQ insert, SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL kill sequence, periodic maintenance-job registration (D-019, D-021, D-022, D-030).

fn-kill_with_sequence.PURPOSE: SIGTERM, wait grace seconds, SIGKILL fallback (D-021)

fn-move_to_dlq.PURPOSE: persist a permanently-failed job into jobs.db.jobs_dlq (D-019)

fn-register_all_retention_jobs.PURPOSE: wire purge-expired-pending + media _staging sweep + retention jobs

type-WikiLockManager.PURPOSE: 3-tier acquire/release: semaphore → asyncio.Lock per WIKI → flock, stale-PID recovery

M-SCHEDULER-FIRING.NAME: Scheduler / job firing
M-SCHEDULER-FIRING.purpose: Cron/date job firing bridge: one-shot reminder (aisw-kcz, Phase-D.a) — create_reminder_job writes a jobs.Job row then registers an APScheduler DateTrigger, fire_job delivers it as a plain Telegram message (no Claude/WIKI); recurring digest (aisw-oqq, Phase-D.b.1) — create_digest_job writes a jobs.Job(kind='digest_job', status='scheduled') then scheduler.add_job(fire_digest_job, CronTrigger, replace_existing=True), fire_digest_job resolves the owner's WIKI set, runs run_wiki_session with extra_add_dirs under a Semaphore + per-WIKI lock, delivers the assistant text via tg/output, and on 3 consecutive failures auto-disables (status='disabled' + remove_job + move_to_dlq, D-019; 600s timeout = Transient strike); both firing callbacks take only a picklable int and read deps from module-level context registries set once at startup (D-002, D-010, D-019, D-022, D-024, D-025, tech-spec §3/§6, ADR-006, ADR-007).
M-SCHEDULER-FIRING.depends: M-STORAGE-JOBS, M-SCHEDULER, M-TG-TEXT, M-WIKI-RUNNER, M-WIKI-LIFECYCLE, M-STORAGE-SESSIONS

fn-set_firing_context.PURPOSE: install the module-level (TgSender, jobs async_sessionmaker) registry for fire_job once at startup

fn-create_reminder_job.PURPOSE: INSERT+commit a jobs.Job(kind='reminder_job', status='pending', scheduled_at_utc, payload=ReminderPayload) then scheduler.add_job(fire_job, DateTrigger, args=[job_id], misfire_grace_time=None); returns job_id

fn-fire_job.PURPOSE: APScheduler callback (picklable int arg): load Job, guard status=='pending' AND user_state not terminal ('done'/'skipped') else skip — the latter suppresses a still-pending reminder the user already resolved via its card (scheduler.reminder.skipped reason='user_resolved', aisw-z0s); mark in_progress → sender.send_message('🔔 Напоминание: …') → done (or failed+last_error); fail-fast if context not set

fn-set_digest_context.PURPOSE: install the module-level digest registry (runner adapter, Semaphore, WikiLockManager, owner-WIKI-set resolver, jobs sessionmaker, audit sessionmaker, TgSender, sessions sessionmaker — the last for user_digest_prefs, aisw-pv8) for fire_digest_job once at startup

fn-create_digest_job.PURPOSE: INSERT+commit a jobs.Job(kind='digest_job', status='scheduled', priority=Lane.DIGEST, payload=DigestPayload(recurrence, wiki_scope:str|list[str], window_hours)) then scheduler.add_job(fire_digest_job, CronTrigger(**recurrence.to_cron(), timezone=rec.tz), args=[job_id], id='digest:<id>', replace_existing=True); commit-before-add_job; logs scheduler.digest.scheduled with wiki_scope; returns job_id (aisw-269: list scope)

fn-fire_digest_job.PURPOSE: APScheduler callback (picklable int arg): load Job, guard status=='scheduled' else skip; resolve owner WIKI set (minus Inbox-WIKI); if payload.wiki_scope is a list intersect by dir-stem (scheduler.digest.scope_filter; empty kept → ru notice, scheduler.digest.delivered empty='scope_vanished', no strike); → primary + extra_add_dirs; assemble prompts/digest.md + jobs.db planner context; read the owner's user_digest_prefs (degrade-to-all-on on any error → scheduler.digest.prefs_read_failed) and, when a section is off, append «Не включай разделы: …» to the planner context + log scheduler.digest.sections_filtered — byte-identical otherwise (aisw-pv8); run_wiki_session under Semaphore + WikiLockManager(primary); deliver via tg/output; success → status stays 'scheduled', retry_count=0; failure/timeout → retry_count++ and at 3 → status='disabled' + remove_job + move_to_dlq; bad payload → 'disabled' + DLQ

fn-list_owner_digest_job_ids.PURPOSE: read-only: the calling owner's enabled (status=='scheduled') digest_job ids — for the /digest_now slash command (aisw-269)

fn-run_section_expand.PURPOSE: re-run Claude scoped to one digest section over the owner's WIKI set via DigestRunner(section=...) (returns text, or None if no WIKI) — for the /expand <section> slash command (aisw-269); ignores user_digest_prefs (an explicit /expand overrides the toggle)

fn-get_owner_digest_prefs.PURPOSE: the owner's DigestPrefs (storage.sessions.digest_prefs.get_digest_prefs over the sessions sessionmaker in the digest context) — for /digest_sections; raises DigestNotInitialisedError if unwired (aisw-pv8)

fn-set_owner_digest_section.PURPOSE: flip one digest section for the owner (storage.sessions.digest_prefs.set_digest_section), returns the new DigestPrefs — for the digestsec: callback; raises DigestNotInitialisedError if unwired (aisw-pv8)

type-DigestRunner.PURPOSE: Protocol — async digest runner; section:str|None=None (None ⇒ full prompts/digest.md, a key ⇒ prompts/digest_expand.md)

type-DigestNotInitialisedError.PURPOSE: raised by fire_digest_job / list_owner_digest_job_ids / run_section_expand / get_owner_digest_prefs / set_owner_digest_section when set_digest_context was never called

M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER.NAME: Scheduler / cron-user firing
M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER.purpose: Cron-user job firing bridge (aisw-02v) — symmetric to M-SCHEDULER-FIRING/digest: create_cron_user_job INSERTs jobs.Job(kind='cron_user', payload=CronUserPayload(recurrence, command, wiki_id?)) and registers an AsyncIOScheduler CronTrigger(**recurrence.to_cron(), timezone=rec.tz) with id='cron_user:<id>'+replace_existing; on fire (picklable int callback) it loads the row, builds a CronUserQueueMsg (job_id, owner_telegram_id, chat_id, command, correlation_id, scheduled_at_utc), pushes to PriorityJobQueue(lane=Lane.CRON_WRITE), and updates status='scheduled'→'queued'. Module-level _ctx registry installed once at startup by set_cron_user_context. Does NOT execute CLI — execution is M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER's concern.

fn-set_cron_user_context.PURPOSE: install the module-level (AsyncIOScheduler, PriorityJobQueue, jobs async_sessionmaker) registry for fire_cron_user_job once at startup

fn-create_cron_user_job.PURPOSE: INSERT+commit a jobs.Job(kind='cron_user', status='scheduled', priority=Lane.CRON_WRITE, payload=CronUserPayload(recurrence, command, wiki_id)) then scheduler.add_job(fire_cron_user_job, CronTrigger(**recurrence.to_cron(), timezone=user_tz), args=[job_id], id=f'cron_user:{job_id}', replace_existing=True); commit-before-add_job; returns job_id (aisw-02v)

fn-fire_cron_user_job.PURPOSE: APScheduler callback (picklable int arg): load Job by id, guard status=='scheduled' else skip (idempotent on replay); construct CronUserQueueMsg(job_id, owner_telegram_id, chat_id, command, correlation_id=uuid4().hex, scheduled_at_utc=now_utc); await queue.put(Lane.CRON_WRITE, msg); UPDATE status='queued'; fail-fast if context not set (aisw-02v)

type-CronUserContextNotInitialisedError.PURPOSE: raised by fire_cron_user_job when set_cron_user_context was never called

M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER.NAME: Scheduler / queue consumer
M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER.purpose: Single async drain loop over PriorityJobQueue (aisw-02v, formerly aisw-cig YAGNI-deferred). CronConsumer.run() awaits queue.get(), then for each item: UPDATE jobs.Job status='running' + started_at_utc; spawn the Claude CLI directly `<claude_binary> --system-prompt <content> --setting-sources "" --disable-slash-commands --permission-mode dontAsk --disallowedTools WebFetch -- <command>` via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec with restricted env (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) and neutral_cwd — NO systemd-run wrapper (aisw-abc/ADR-010: simple single-user model, parity with M-WIKI-RUNNER); wait_for(communicate, timeout_s=600); on TimeoutError → kill_with_sequence (D-021) + ru error message; on exit=0 → ChainSplitter chunked bot.send_message; on exit!=0 → "❌ Ошибка ({code}): {truncate_stderr}". Updates Job to terminal status ('finished'|'failed'). Single-task wired in __main__ (D-011 §3 lane=CRON_WRITE, NFR-1 concurrency=1).
M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER.depends: M-SCHEDULER, M-STORAGE-JOBS, M-TG-TEXT, M-CLAUDE-CLI-COMMON, M-LLM-FAILOVER, M-LLM-CODEX

fn-CronConsumer.__init__.PURPOSE: constructor DI: queue, bot, claude_binary, claude_config_dir, prompt_path, jobs_session_maker, timeout_s=600, spawner (R-1 mitigation: bot is injected, not module-global)

fn-CronConsumer.run.PURPOSE: async drain loop — `while True: item = await queue.get(); try: await self._execute_one(item); except asyncio.CancelledError: raise; except Exception: log scheduler.consumer.unexpected, continue`; one item at a time (NFR-1 at-most-once / concurrency=1)

fn-CronConsumer._execute_one.PURPOSE: per-item executor: validate payload via CronUserQueueMsg.model_validate; UPDATE Job→'running'; build argv via claude_cli.common primitives (direct claude invocation, no systemd-run); spawn + wait_for(600s); chunk stdout via ChainSplitter; bot.send_message * n; terminal status; emit log anchors at every decision point

type-CronConsumer.PURPOSE: cron-user queue drain loop; constructor-DI for bot/queue/jobs sessionmaker; .run() is the asyncio.Task entrypoint; cancellable for shutdown

type-_QUEUE_GET_TIMEOUT_S.PURPOSE: internal constant — None (blocking get); kept as a named symbol for future bounded-poll if shutdown semantics ever need it

M-TG-CRON-ADD.NAME: Telegram / /cron_add command
M-TG-CRON-ADD.purpose: aiogram Router add-on (aisw-02v) — registers the `/cron_add <NL recurrence> | <command>` Command handler. Splits CommandObject.args on the first `|`; calls classifier.recurrence.parse_recurrence(schedule_text, user_tz=resolve_tz(owner)); on RecurrenceParseResult.escalate or empty command → reply usage hint (ru); on parsed Recurrence → scheduler.cron_user.create_cron_user_job(owner_telegram_id, chat_id, recurrence, command, user_tz, wiki_id=None) → reply "✅ Запланировано (id={job_id}): {humanized}. Команда: {preview}". No interactive confirm in walking skeleton (AD-02): parser is rule-based + reply renders parsed cron in ru for visual verification.

fn-register_cron_add_handlers.PURPOSE: factory registering the /cron_add Command handler on a caller-owned aiogram.Router; takes a get_user_tz: Callable[[int], Awaitable[str]] for IANA tz resolution (R-4 mitigation: handler rejects parser.escalate with usage hint, no silent bad-cron)

fn-_handle_cron_add.PURPOSE: async handler: split args on first '|'; parse_recurrence; escalate → usage; empty command → usage; parsed → create_cron_user_job → answer with humanized ru recurrence + job_id

fn-_humanize_recurrence.PURPOSE: pure: Recurrence → ru string ('каждый день в 09:00 (Europe/Moscow)' | 'каждую среду в 14:00 (Europe/Moscow)' | 'каждое 5-е число в 10:00 (Europe/Moscow)')

type-CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU.PURPOSE: ru usage string for parse failures / empty input

type-CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU_HINT.PURPOSE: ru hint with examples (каждый день в 9, каждую среду в 14:00, каждого 5го в 10:00)

M-CLASSIFIER-STAGE0.NAME: Classifier / Stage-0
M-CLASSIFIER-STAGE0.purpose: Stage-0 Haiku intent classifier — prompt cache (semver+sha256), backend abstraction (Claude CLI default / Anthropic API optional / Fake), Pydantic intent schema, NL time parser (dateparser → Haiku-fallback → escalate) (D-009, D-010, D-013, D-015).

fn-classify.PURPOSE: Stage-0 orchestrator: backend call + Pydantic validation + audit upsert + structlog

fn-parse_time.PURPOSE: NL time parser: dateparser → Haiku-fallback → escalate (D-010); prefer_future kwarg threads PREFER_DATES_FROM='future' for reminders (aisw-kcz)

type-Intent.PURPOSE: closed enum of Stage-0 intents (D-009, mapped to spec §3 job kinds)

type-ClassifierResult.PURPOSE: frozen Pydantic v2 result with intent/confidence/audit fields

type-TimeParseResult.PURPOSE: frozen Pydantic v2 NL-time result with when_utc + escalate flag

type-ClassifierBackend.PURPOSE: Protocol; ClaudeCliBackend default, AnthropicApiBackend optional, FakeClaudeRunner test double

type-Spawner.PURPOSE: subprocess spawn primitive seam (chunk 16 injects systemd-run prefix)

M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE.NAME: Classifier / recurrence parsing
M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE.purpose: NL recurrence parser for the digest fast-path (aisw-oqq, Inbox-WIKI Phase-D.b.1): parse_recurrence turns ru phrasings («каждый день в 9», «по будням в 19:00», «еженедельно по понедельникам и пятницам в 8») into a Recurrence value (kind daily|weekly, time_hhmm, weekdays tuple, tz) with to_cron() → APScheduler CronTrigger kwargs; conservative rule-based regex + optional Haiku fallback (same shape as time_parse); escalate on monthly/interval/raw-cron/ambiguous (D-002, tech-spec §3).

fn-parse_recurrence.PURPOSE: ru NL → RecurrenceParseResult: rule-based regex (daily / weekly-by-weekdays) → optional Haiku fallback → escalate; conservative, escalates on monthly/interval/cron-string

type-Recurrence.PURPOSE: frozen Pydantic v2 value: kind Literal[daily,weekly], time_hhmm, weekdays tuple[int,...], tz; to_cron() → CronTrigger kwargs (daily→hour/minute, weekly→day_of_week comma-joined+hour/minute)

type-RecurrenceParseResult.PURPOSE: frozen Pydantic v2 result: recurrence | None + escalate flag + reason

M-WIKI-RUNNER.NAME: WIKI / Stage-1 runner
M-WIKI-RUNNER.purpose: Stage-1a/1b Sonnet runner orchestrator — assemble prompt (base+overlay+per-WIKI CLAUDE.md), acquire WIKI lock, spawn `claude --output-format stream-json`, parse stream events, hard timeout + kill-sequence, aggregate assistant text (D-007, D-011, D-012, D-021).

fn-run_wiki_session.PURPOSE: public entrypoint orchestrating one Stage-1a/1b run; extra_add_dirs kwarg appends read-only --add-dir paths after the primary WIKI (digest multi-WIKI, aisw-oqq), alongside media_dirs

fn-assemble_prompt.PURPOSE: concat base+overlay (+per-WIKI CLAUDE.md if present) → atomic write

fn-aggregate_text.PURPOSE: extract assistant text from WikiRunResult.events (stream-json shapes)

fn-parse_stream_json.PURPOSE: async iterator over an asyncio.StreamReader → StreamEvent

type-WikiRunResult.PURPOSE: dataclass result of one run (run_id, exit_code, events, …)

type-StreamEvent.PURPOSE: frozen Pydantic v2 model for one stream-json line (type+payload)

type-Spawner.PURPOSE: Protocol; spawn(argv, env, cwd) → SpawnedProcess; AsyncioSpawner default

M-WIKI-MIGRATION.NAME: WIKI schema generation
M-WIKI-MIGRATION.purpose: Generate, validate, and apply a tailored managed WIKI schema with Claude and safe Codex text fallback.

type-SchemaGenerator.PURPOSE: Protocol returning managed-zone text before any file mutation.

type-ClaudeCliSchemaGenerator.PURPOSE: Claude-first text generator with safe Codex fallback.

fn-apply_generated_schema.PURPOSE: Apply only validated text; preserve the default schema on failure.

M-WIKI-LIFECYCLE.NAME: WIKI / lifecycle
M-WIKI-LIFECYCLE.purpose: WIKI name normalisation (Cyrillic→Latin ISO 9, PascalCase), owner-scoped create/lookup/soft-delete/restore with anti-spam cap + near-duplicate detection, CLAUDE.md frontmatter + managed/user-zone markers + template loader, 5-step pre-flight grounding (D-008, D-039, D-041).

fn-normalize_wiki_name.PURPOSE: raw NL string → WikiName (primary, hyphenated_lookup, slug)

type-AntiSpamCapError.PURPOSE: hard cap reached on wiki creation

type-Frontmatter.PURPOSE: CLAUDE.md frontmatter model; managed/user HTML zone markers

M-WIKI-SCOPE.NAME: WIKI / adaptive scope resolution for group-2 intents
M-WIKI-SCOPE.purpose: Scope resolution for group-2 (adaptive) intents — wiki_query and digest — which, unlike wiki_ingest, had no mechanism to decide "one WIKI or several" and always ran cross-WIKI at the user root with no domain CLAUDE.md/Data-layout context (root cause of aisw-o6m: a calorie query missed diet/food_log.csv). resolve_query_scope reuses the prod ingest fast-path thresholds (hint_match.is_confident, MIN_SCORE/MIN_MARGIN/MAX_FASTPATH_CHARS) to decide a confident single-domain wiki_query should run scoped inside that WIKI (Phase B); collect_layouts renders a bounded ru "Карта WIKI пользователя" block from each WIKI's managed CLAUDE.md zone, injected into cross-WIKI wiki_query and digest runs so the model sees every WIKI's Data layout (Phase A). Adapter-side only (ADR-034) — the WikiRunner Protocol is unchanged; M-RUNTIME-WIRING's _WikiRunnerAdapter/_DigestRunnerAdapter call in and degrade to legacy cross-root behaviour on any resolver error (FR-6).

fn-resolve_query_scope.PURPOSE: (text, catalog, wikis) -> ScopeDecision; cross unless hint_match.is_confident AND text within MAX_FASTPATH_CHARS AND the matched stem has a wiki dir

fn-collect_layouts.PURPOSE: (stem, wiki dir) pairs -> ru layouts block from each WIKI's managed CLAUDE.md zone (MANAGED_START/MANAGED_END), per-WIKI excerpt capped at MAX_LAYOUT_CHARS_PER_WIKI; '' when wikis is empty; missing file/markers/empty body -> '(схема не описана)' fallback per WIKI

type-ScopeDecision.PURPOSE: frozen result: kind Literal['scoped','cross'], target_stem/target_path (None on cross), top_score, margin

type-MAX_LAYOUT_CHARS_PER_WIKI.PURPOSE: per-WIKI excerpt cap (4000) guarding the prompt budget against a pathological hand-edited CLAUDE.md

M-INBOX.NAME: Inbox-WIKI materialise + hint cache
M-INBOX.purpose: Per-user Inbox-WIKI/ first-contact materialise, "## Inbox hint" Markdown parser + sessions.inbox_hint_cache repo (stat→cache-hit hot path), deterministic hint-catalog token-overlap scorer for the pre-router fast-path, media staging+promotion for voice/photo ingest (D-004, D-016, D-022).
M-INBOX.path: src/ai_steward_wiki/inbox/__init__.py, src/ai_steward_wiki/inbox/materialize.py, src/ai_steward_wiki/inbox/parser.py, src/ai_steward_wiki/inbox/hint_cache.py, src/ai_steward_wiki/inbox/hint_match.py, src/ai_steward_wiki/inbox/staging.py

fn-ensure_inbox_wiki.PURPOSE: idempotent first-contact materialise; returns Path to Inbox-WIKI dir

fn-extract_inbox_hint.PURPOSE: parse CLAUDE.md text, return trimmed '## Inbox hint' body or None

fn-get_or_refresh_hint.PURPOSE: hot-path helper: stat→cache-hit on (size,mtime) else read+sha256

fn-score_catalog.PURPOSE: token-overlap count of message vs each domain's hint (+stem name); ranked HintMatch (aisw-5sd)

fn-is_confident.PURPOSE: True iff one domain ≥MIN_SCORE matched keywords AND ≥MIN_MARGIN lead over #2 — fires the router-bypass (aisw-5sd)

type-HintMatch.PURPOSE: frozen scorer result: ranked ((stem,score),…), top_stem, top_score, margin

fn-sweep_staging.PURPOSE: delete staging files older than ttl_s in one inbox_root; returns count (D-022)

fn-sweep_all_user_staging.PURPOSE: run sweep_staging on every <wiki_root>/<user>/Inbox-WIKI (per-user sweep, D-022)

type-MediaRef.PURPOSE: dataclass (staging_path, sha256, mime, ext, size, run_id)

M-INBOX-ROUTER.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Stage-1a router reply parser
M-INBOX-ROUTER.purpose: Parse the Stage-1a Inbox-WIKI Router reply (a fenced ```router block) into a RouterDecision; tolerant — falls back to CLARIFY on a missing/malformed block (aisw-dsg, Inbox-WIKI Phase-A; D-004, D-016, prompts/inbox.md ≥1.1.0).

type-RouterIntent.PURPOSE: closed enum: route | create_wiki | clarify | reject

type-RouterDecision.PURPOSE: frozen Pydantic model (intent, target_wiki, notes, raw, parsed_ok)

type-RouterError.PURPOSE: raised by the runtime adapter when the Router CLI run fails unrecoverably

fn-parse_router_reply.PURPOSE: extract the last ```router block, parse key:value, normalise cross-field, fallback to CLARIFY

M-INBOX-ROUTE.NAME: Inbox-WIKI route+ingest helpers
M-INBOX-ROUTE.purpose: Resolve/create the target <Domain>-WIKI from a RouterDecision (lookup-or-create; AntiSpamCapError/WikiNameError → RouteRejection), stage the raw payload into it (write raw/<ts>_<source>.md + promote media via promote_path_to_raw), pick the Stage-1b domain overlay, build the librarian ingest prompt, and (Phase-C, aisw-e45) (de)serialise a route+ingest action to/from a JSON-able dict for the confirm-loop draft (aisw-zd9 Phase-B + aisw-e45 Phase-C; D-004, D-023, smart-inbox-routing).

type-RouteRejection.PURPOSE: cap / bad_name rejection with a ru hint

type-RouteAction.PURPOSE: a staged route+ingest action awaiting user confirmation (decision + user_text + source + media_paths + correlation_id) — Phase-C

fn-resolve_target_wiki.PURPOSE: lookup-or-create the target <Domain>-WIKI for the owner; ROUTE-missing → create + on_route_missing callback

fn-stage_raw_into_wiki.PURPOSE: write raw/<ts>_<source>.md + promote media into <wiki>/raw/media/ (skip already-gone media)

fn-pick_domain_overlay.PURPOSE: prompts/domain-<slug>.md if it exists else domain-default.md

fn-build_ingest_prompt.PURPOSE: ru Stage-1b ingest instruction referencing the raw path(s) + the user text

fn-route_action_to_payload.PURPOSE: serialise a RouteAction to a JSON-able dict for pending_confirms.draft_json (Phase-C)

fn-route_action_from_payload.PURPOSE: inverse: reconstruct a typed RouteAction (RouterDecision/Path) from the draft dict (Phase-C)

M-INGEST-IDEM.NAME: Ingest idempotency
M-INGEST-IDEM.purpose: Two-layer ingest dedup (D-018): L1 TG update_id (24h) + L2 SHA-256 content hash (30d) with NFKC text normalisation; records dedup outcomes into audit.db.

fn-normalize_text.PURPOSE: NFKC + strip + lower + collapse-whitespace for L2 text hashing

fn-compute_content_hash.PURPOSE: SHA-256 hex of normalized text or raw bytes per ContentKind

type-SeenFileMatch.PURPOSE: dataclass returned on L2 collision (hash + first-seen ts + owner)

M-OPS-PII.NAME: Ops / PII + retention
M-OPS-PII.purpose: Tiered PII redactor (NIST SP 800-122) for structlog + DB write paths, GDPR purge_user admin endpoint (allow-listed stores), APScheduler retention jobs implementing §10.4 retention table + _trash/_staging sweeps (D-034 §10.4).

type-PIIRedactor.PURPOSE: stateful redactor with HMAC secret + on/off toggles; make_structlog_processor adapter

fn-purge_user.PURPOSE: GDPR endpoint; admin-gated; allow-listed stores per D-034 §10.4

type-RetentionPolicy.PURPOSE: Pydantic v2 model declaring one §10.4 retention rule

fn-run_purge.PURPOSE: execute one retention policy; returns PurgeResult

fn-register_retention_jobs.PURPOSE: add all RETENTION_POLICIES to a scheduler

M-OPS-BACKUP.NAME: Ops / backup + per-WIKI git
M-OPS-BACKUP.purpose: Daily db_snapshot maintenance job (VACUUM INTO state/snapshots/<UTC>/ for jobs/audit/sessions, 7d rolling retention) and per-WIKI local git auto-commit helpers (D-037, tech-spec §10.2/§10.5).

fn-snapshot_databases.PURPOSE: run VACUUM INTO for each configured DB into per-day dir (0700)

fn-register_db_snapshot_job.PURPOSE: register cron 03:00 UTC daily db_snapshot job

fn-init_wiki_git.PURPOSE: idempotent git init + .gitignore writer per WIKI dir (D-037)

fn-auto_commit.PURPOSE: stage-all + commit; no-op when nothing staged

type-SnapshotResult.PURPOSE: Pydantic v2 frozen model with per-store outcomes

M-MIGRATION.NAME: Migration ETL package
M-MIGRATION.purpose: One-shot ETL package that migrates legacy ai-steward bot data (flat {User}/{Project}/ tree, planner.json + raw files) into ai-steward-wiki (SQLite jobs.db + per-WIKI Karpathy layout). Read-only on the source snapshot; writes target DBs and FS only with --execute (default --dry-run). Public surface = the CLI in __main__ plus the LoadReport / TargetPlan dataclasses re-exported for testability (aisw-0a5).
M-MIGRATION.depends: M-AUTH-USERS, M-STORAGE-JOBS, M-WIKI-LIFECYCLE, M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE, M-MIGRATION-CONFIG, M-MIGRATION-EXTRACT, M-MIGRATION-TRANSFORM, M-MIGRATION-LOAD, M-MIGRATION-REPORT, M-MIGRATION-CLI

M-MIGRATION-CLI.NAME: Migration ETL / CLI entrypoint
M-MIGRATION-CLI.purpose: argparse-based CLI entrypoint for the migration ETL — parses argv, dispatches extract → transform → (dry-run report | execute + report). Fail-Fast: any unexpected exception aborts the run; pre-step jobs.db snapshot is the rollback artefact. Invocation: `uv run python -m ai_steward_wiki.migration --help` (aisw-0a5).
M-MIGRATION-CLI.depends: M-MIGRATION-CONFIG, M-MIGRATION-EXTRACT, M-MIGRATION-TRANSFORM, M-MIGRATION-LOAD, M-MIGRATION-REPORT, M-FOUNDATION-LOGGING, M-STORAGE-JOBS

M-MIGRATION-CONFIG.NAME: Migration ETL / mapping table
M-MIGRATION-CONFIG.purpose: Hardcoded mapping table from legacy {User}/{Project}/ tree to new <wiki_root>/<owner>/<Name>-WIKI/ layout. Single SSoT for identity + domain mapping per Q2/Q3 discovery decisions and D-042 identity vocabulary (aisw-0a5).

type-UserMapping.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: telegram_id, source_dir, display_name, role, tz, lang, projects

type-ProjectMapping.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: source_project (or None for root), target_wiki, template_id

const-DROP_DIRS.PURPOSE: frozenset of dir names that must never be migrated (dev/skeleton)

const-DROP_FILE_PATTERNS.PURPOSE: file glob patterns dropped during walk

M-MIGRATION-EXTRACT.NAME: Migration ETL / Phase E (extract)
M-MIGRATION-EXTRACT.purpose: Phase E of ETL: read snapshot dir (rsync of /home/bgs/ai-steward/), parse planner.json files, walk file trees, classify files. No transformation logic — only read + classify into in-memory SourceUserData. Pure side-effect-free w.r.t. target paths (aisw-0a5).

type-SourcePlannerItem.PURPOSE: one parsed item from planner.json (frozen Pydantic)

type-SourceFile.PURPOSE: one file with classified location + type

fn-extract_user.PURPOSE: compose parse + walk for one UserMapping

M-MIGRATION-TRANSFORM.NAME: Migration ETL / Phase T (transform)
M-MIGRATION-TRANSFORM.purpose: Pure transformation layer of ETL: SourceUserData → TargetPlan. All decision-making for active-item filtering, MSK→UTC TZ conversion, category mapping, recipients fail-fast, remind_before fan-out, monthly→cron_user workaround, daily/weekly→cron_user with Recurrence, file routing to <wiki>/<sub>/ vs <wiki>/raw/<sub>/, frontmatter rendering. No FS or DB writes — only in-memory PlannedJob/PlannedFileCopy/PlannedLegacyDoc dataclasses (aisw-0a5).

type-PlannedJob.PURPOSE: one row that will be INSERTed into jobs.db

type-PlannedLegacyDoc.PURPOSE: one rendered Markdown file (legacy CLAUDE.md or planner-history)

type-PlannedWiki.PURPOSE: one WIKI to bootstrap (owner, raw_name, template_id)

type-TargetPlan.PURPOSE: composite of all the above, per-user grouped

fn-extract_chat_id.PURPOSE: recipients list[int] → single int, fail-fast on len != 1

fn-monthly_to_cron.PURPOSE: {type:monthly, day, time} → 'M H D * *' (validated via APS)

fn-render_legacy_claude_md.PURPOSE: legacy CLAUDE.md → rendered Markdown for migrated WIKI

M-MIGRATION-LOAD.NAME: Migration ETL / Phase L (load)
M-MIGRATION-LOAD.purpose: Side-effect layer of ETL. Takes a TargetPlan and applies it to the running system: writes users.toml, bootstraps WIKIs via WikiLifecycleManager, copies files (shutil.copy2 preserving mtime), writes generated Markdown docs, INSERTs Job rows into jobs.db in a single transaction. dry_run=True makes every op a no-op (only logs and counts). Pre-step jobs.db snapshot acts as rollback artefact (aisw-0a5).

type-MigrationLoader.PURPOSE: orchestrates write_users_toml, bootstrap_wikis, copy_files, write_legacy_docs, insert_jobs, snapshot_db

M-MIGRATION-REPORT.NAME: Migration ETL / report renderer
M-MIGRATION-REPORT.purpose: Render a Markdown migration_report.md summarising what the ETL did (or would do, in --dry-run mode). Inputs are the TargetPlan and optional LoadReport (post-execute counters). The report is the single user-facing artefact for cutover review (aisw-0a5).

M-TG-TEXT.NAME: Telegram I/O text layer
M-TG-TEXT.purpose: aiogram 3 dispatcher + allowlist outer-middleware, graduated 3-tier confirmations (auto/implicit/explicit + 10min TTL), D-025 output-size hybrid policy (≤3500 inline / ≤10000 chain-split / >10000 summary) + persistence, D-026 HTML-balanced throttled streaming edits (D-023, D-024, D-025, D-026, D-031, D-042).

fn-build_bot.PURPOSE: construct aiogram.Bot with HTML default parse_mode

fn-deliver_output.PURPOSE: D-025 hybrid size policy + persistence; returns DeliveryReceipt

type-AiogramSender.PURPOSE: adapter wrapping aiogram.Bot to satisfy the TgSender Protocol

type-AllowlistMiddleware.PURPOSE: aiogram outer-middleware gating updates by telegram_id (D-031)

type-ConfirmationService.PURPOSE: graduated auto/implicit/explicit confirmation flow + 10min TTL (D-023); request_explicit takes a keyboard_factory (3-button default; route confirms pass build_route_confirm_keyboard)

fn-build_route_confirm_keyboard.PURPOSE: 2-button (Подтвердить/Отмена) inline keyboard for Inbox-WIKI route confirms (Phase-C, aisw-e45)

type-HtmlBalancer.PURPOSE: tokeniser-based open/close balancer for whitelisted TG HTML tags

type-ChainSplitter.PURPOSE: split text into ≤3 parts at semantic boundaries with (i/M) footer

type-StreamEditor.PURPOSE: D-026 throttled streaming edits: 1.5s OR Δ≥50 + chain-split at 4000 + final-flush

type-HaikuSummarizer.PURPOSE: Protocol for >10000-char summary path; LengthCapSummarizer safe fallback

M-TG-VOICE.NAME: Telegram / voice STT
M-TG-VOICE.purpose: Voice (ogg/opus) ingestion — faster-whisper CPU STT (CTranslate2 int8, ru/en, RTF ≤ 0.5) + staging hand-off (D-022, tech-spec §9). audio and video_note also route here.

type-FasterWhisperTranscriber.PURPOSE: default impl, lazy CTranslate2 int8 CPU model load, fallback lang ru

type-Transcript.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass (text, lang, duration_s, model, rtf)

type-VoiceUnavailableError.PURPOSE: raised when faster-whisper STT backend is missing

M-TG-PHOTO.NAME: Telegram / photo ingest
M-TG-PHOTO.purpose: Photo ingestion — stage bytes to _staging/, return a MediaRef; photos (and image-documents) run Claude vision via the wiki pipeline (D-022, tech-spec §9).

type-PhotoIngestor.PURPOSE: stages photo bytes; returns MediaRef for the wiki pipeline

type-PHOTO_MIME_TO_EXT.PURPOSE: closed mapping of allowed photo MIME types → extension

M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER.NAME: Ingest pipeline / classify→run→deliver
M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER.purpose: DefaultPipeline coordinator over already-built ingest blocks — L1/L2 dedup, Stage-0 classify → Stage-1 run → deliver_output; voice transcript path; photo→vision via media_paths; document mime router (PDF text extract, size cap); Inbox-WIKI routing: a ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI RouterDecision is proposed via an inline-button route confirm (persisted as a route_ingest pending row) and the Stage-1b move+ingest runs in on_confirm_callback on "confirmed" (Phase-C, aisw-e45); a Stage-0 intent=reminder fast-path (BEFORE the Stage-1a router) parses NL time (prefer_future), proposes a one-shot reminder via the same inline-button confirm and on "confirmed" calls scheduler.firing.create_reminder_job — recurring-digest phrasing answered with a ru "not yet" line (Phase-D.a, aisw-kcz); a '## Inbox hint' fast-path (also BEFORE the Stage-1a router, when a hint_catalog_resolver is wired) scores the message against the sender's cached hint catalog and on a confident single match (inbox.hint_match.is_confident) synthesises a RouterDecision(ROUTE) → the same route_ingest confirm loop, skipping the heavy Sonnet Router; miss/ambiguous/empty/disabled ⇒ falls through unchanged (Phase-E.b, aisw-5sd); Russian safe-fallback acks (chunk 20, D-022, D-023).
M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER.depends: M-CLASSIFIER-STAGE0, M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE, M-WIKI-RUNNER, M-TG-TEXT, M-INGEST-IDEM, M-TG-VOICE, M-TG-PHOTO, M-INBOX, M-INBOX-ROUTER, M-INBOX-ROUTE, M-SCHEDULER-FIRING

type-MessagePipeline.PURPOSE: Protocol for the 5 handler entry points (on_text/on_voice/on_photo/on_document/on_confirm_callback)

type-Router.PURPOSE: narrow Inbox-WIKI Stage-1a router wrapper Protocol (aisw-dsg); intercepts WIKI_INGEST/WIKI_QUERY/UNKNOWN

type-Librarian.PURPOSE: narrow Inbox-WIKI Stage-1b librarian wrapper Protocol (aisw-zd9); executes ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI → IngestOutcome

type-IngestOutcome.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass returned by Librarian.ingest (status ok|rejected|run_failed, reply, run_id, target_wiki, created)

type-WikiRunner.PURPOSE: narrow Stage-1a/1b wrapper Protocol; WikiRunOutcome result dataclass

fn-_handle_reminder_confirm.PURPOSE: on_confirm_callback branch for category=='reminder' pending rows: resolve race-safely → on 'confirmed' rebuild the draft + scheduler.firing.create_reminder_job + ack; cancel/stale → ru notice (aisw-kcz)

fn-build_reminder_recap.PURPOSE: ru recap line 'Поставлю напоминание на DD.MM HH:MM (TZ): «message». Подтверждаешь?' for the reminder confirm draft (aisw-kcz)

fn-_handle_digest_intent.PURPOSE: recurring-digest fast-path: parse_recurrence → on escalate ru clarification; aisw-269 — if owner_wikis_resolver wired, extract_wiki_names(text, owner-stems): a «по <Name>» token matching no WIKI → ru clarification (tg.pipeline.digest.wiki_unknown), no draft; else build a category='digest' confirm draft (recurrence, wiki_scope:'all'|list[str], window_hours=24) + recap and ConfirmationService.request_explicit

fn-_handle_digest_confirm.PURPOSE: on_confirm_callback branch for category=='digest': resolve race-safely → on 'confirmed' rebuild the draft + scheduler.firing.create_digest_job (wiki_scope passed through as 'all'|list[str]) + ack (names the WIKIs when scoped); cancel/stale → ru notice (aisw-oqq; aisw-269 scope)

fn-build_digest_recap.PURPOSE: ru recap line for the digest confirm draft; appends 'по WIKI: <list>' when wiki_scope is a list (aisw-oqq; +wiki_scope aisw-269)

fn-extract_wiki_names.PURPOSE: heuristic WIKI-name extraction for the digest fast-path: whole-token case-insensitive ∩ with the owner's *-WIKI/ dir-stems → list[str]; none mentioned → 'all'; a capitalised token after «по …» matching no stem → None (caller clarifies) (aisw-269)

M-TG-PIPELINE-STREAMING.NAME: Ingest pipeline / slow-path streaming
M-TG-PIPELINE-STREAMING.purpose: DefaultStreamingDelivery — race the Stage-1 run against a short timer; on slow runs post a placeholder and feed buffered+live stream events into a StreamEditor, finalising with the aggregated text (chunk 21, D-026).

type-StreamingDelivery.PURPOSE: Protocol for the slow-path race+stream wrapper

CrossLink.relation: L1 dedup via IdempotencyService.check_update_id
reply via TgSender (AiogramSender)
thin handlers delegate to MessagePipeline (DefaultPipeline)
VoiceHandler for STT staging — voice, audio and video_note all route to on_voice
PhotoIngestor; photos run Claude vision via the wiki pipeline (media_paths)

/digest_now → firing.list_owner_digest_job_ids + firing.fire_digest_job per row; /expand → firing.run_section_expand (aisw-269); /digest_sections + digestsec: callback → firing.get_owner_digest_prefs / firing.set_owner_digest_section (aisw-pv8)
imports DigestPrefs / TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS / SECTION_DISPLAY_NAME from storage.sessions.digest_prefs for the /digest_sections inline keyboard (aisw-pv8)
reads/writes user_digest_prefs via storage.sessions.digest_prefs (fire_digest_job honours the toggles; get/set_owner_digest_section accessors) — aisw-pv8
constructs DefaultPipeline + include_router via build_dispatcher(pipeline=...)
reads Settings (tokens, db urls, users_toml_path, wiki_root, voice_*, photo_*)
loads users.toml then sync_to_sessions_db + replace_global
alembic upgrade head + build_engine
alembic upgrade head + build_engine
alembic upgrade head + build_engine + build_sessionmaker
constructs VoiceHandler(FasterWhisperTranscriber) when voice_enabled
constructs PhotoIngestor when photo_enabled
register_all_retention_jobs(wiki_root_for_media_sweep) → per-user staging sweep over <wiki_root>/<user>/Inbox-WIKI + promote_path_to_raw after a successful Stage-1 run (D-022; aisw-12t)
reads log_level
syncs users.toml snapshot to sessions.users table
uses apply_sqlite_pragmas in build_engine
uses apply_sqlite_pragmas in build_engine
uses apply_sqlite_pragmas in build_engine
build_scheduler + WikiLockManager + register_all_retention_jobs
builds ClaudeCliBackend/AnthropicApiBackend + PromptCache; _ClassifierAdapter calls classify()
_WikiRunnerAdapter.run → run_wiki_session via AsyncioSpawner + WikiLockAdapter
_WikiRunnerAdapter.run resolves wiki_query scope via resolve_query_scope; both adapters inject collect_layouts for cross-WIKI/multi-WIKI runs (aisw-o6m, ADR-034)
build_bot/build_dispatcher + deliver_output via _OutputDeliveryAdapter
constructs DefaultPipeline (+ DefaultStreamingDelivery)
PIIRedactor structlog processor + register_all_retention_jobs
pending_users CRUD + pending_confirms elevation rows
admin_events elevation trail (D-028)
SQLAlchemyJobStore on jobs.db; move_to_dlq writes jobs_dlq
ClaudeCliBackend uses resolve_binary/build_env/neutral_cwd/system_prompt_argv
record_prompt_version upsert into audit.prompt_versions (D-015)
assemble_prompt + restricted env/cwd for the `claude` subprocess
WikiLockAdapter wraps WikiLockManager; kill_with_sequence on timeout
owner-scoped wiki records + trashed-wiki retention
acquires the WIKI lock during create/migrate
inbox_hint_cache repo over sessions.inbox_hint_cache
L1 tg_updates + L2 seen_files / dedup_hits tables
retention purge of audit.db tables (§10.4)
retention purge of jobs.db tables (§10.4)
register_retention_jobs adds §10.4 cron jobs
purge_staging wraps inbox.staging.sweep_staging
register_db_snapshot_job adds the daily 03:00 UTC VACUUM INTO job
AllowlistMiddleware gates updates by Allowlist (D-031)
deliver_output persists RunOutput index (D-025); chat_logs
ConfirmationService persists pending_confirms (D-023)
VoiceHandler stages bytes via inbox.staging.stage_media (D-022)
PhotoIngestor stages bytes via inbox.staging.stage_media (D-022)
Classifier Protocol → classify()
WikiRunner Protocol → run_wiki_session()
OutputDelivery Protocol → deliver_output(); ConfirmationService.request_explicit + build_route_confirm_keyboard for the Inbox-WIKI route confirm loop (aisw-e45)
L1/L2 dedup via IdempotencyService
voice transcript path → on_voice
photo→vision via media_paths
inbox_wiki_path(telegram_id, wiki_root) resolves the per-sender media-staging root passed to VoiceHandler/PhotoIngestor.handle (aisw-12t, D-022)
Router Protocol returns RouterDecision; RouterError → safe ack (aisw-dsg)
_RouterAdapter parses run_wiki_session reply via parse_router_reply; raises RouterError (aisw-dsg)
_RouterAdapter calls ensure_inbox_wiki before each Stage-1a router run (aisw-dsg)
Librarian Protocol returns IngestOutcome for ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI decisions (aisw-zd9); route_action_to_payload/route_action_from_payload (de)serialise the confirm-loop draft (aisw-e45)
ChatLogPort: persist in/out turns (write_in after STT/OCR, write_out on final reply) + read the last-20/24h window folded into build_router_input (D-033, aisw-kml)
ActiveWikiPort: set_active on a successful route/ingest; get_active (24h TTL) default-routes a cold CLARIFY/REJECT follow-up into the last-active WIKI (aisw-0ym)
_LibrarianAdapter calls resolve_target_wiki/stage_raw_into_wiki/pick_domain_overlay/build_ingest_prompt (aisw-zd9)
resolve_target_wiki wraps WikiLifecycleManager.lookup/create_wiki (AntiSpamCapError/WikiNameError)
stage_raw_into_wiki promotes media via inbox.staging.promote_path_to_raw
consumes RouterDecision / RouterIntent
wraps the run→deliver slow path
feeds stream events into StreamEditor (D-026)
consumes on_event stream callback from run_wiki_session
create_reminder_job INSERTs a jobs.Job(kind='reminder_job', payload=ReminderPayload); fire_job loads it by id and updates status
create_reminder_job registers a DateTrigger on the AsyncIOScheduler (picklable int args); priority=Lane.USER_WRITE
fire_job delivers the reminder via the TgSender Protocol (AiogramSender)
the reminder confirm callback calls create_reminder_job on 'confirmed'
reminder fast-path calls parse_time(..., prefer_future=True) (aisw-kcz)
firing.set_firing_context(sender, jobs_session_maker) after scheduler.start(); add_listener(_on_job_missed, EVENT_JOB_MISSED); passes jobs sessionmaker + scheduler to DefaultPipeline
parse_recurrence's optional Haiku fallback uses the Stage-0 backend / CLI envelope
default_user_tz fallback for the recurrence timezone
the digest fast-path calls parse_recurrence (aisw-oqq)
the digest confirm callback calls create_digest_job on 'confirmed' (aisw-oqq)
fire_digest_job runs run_wiki_session(..., extra_add_dirs=[...]) under a Semaphore + WikiLockManager (aisw-oqq)
fire_digest_job resolves the owner's <Name>-WIKI set (minus Inbox-WIKI) for the --add-dir list (aisw-oqq)
create_digest_job INSERTs a jobs.Job(kind='digest_job', payload=DigestPayload); fire_digest_job loads it, updates status/retry_count, and on 3 strikes calls move_to_dlq (aisw-oqq)

create_digest_job registers a CronTrigger on the AsyncIOScheduler (picklable int args, replace_existing); priority=Lane.DIGEST; fire_digest_job uses FailureCounter semantics + move_to_dlq + remove_job (aisw-oqq)
_RecurrenceParserAdapter binds parse_recurrence with the Stage-0 backend + prompts/recurrence.md (aisw-oqq)
binds _resolve_owner_wikis into firing.set_digest_context (aisw-oqq)
create_cron_user_job INSERTs a jobs.Job(kind='cron_user', payload=CronUserPayload(recurrence, command, wiki_id?)); fire_cron_user_job loads by id and updates status='scheduled'→'queued' (aisw-02v)

create_cron_user_job registers a CronTrigger on the AsyncIOScheduler (picklable int args, replace_existing); priority=Lane.CRON_WRITE; fire_cron_user_job pushes CronUserQueueMsg onto PriorityJobQueue (aisw-02v)
create_cron_user_job consumes a Recurrence value and calls .to_cron() for CronTrigger kwargs (aisw-02v)
awaits PriorityJobQueue.get(); uses kill_with_sequence (D-021) for systemd-run scope timeout (aisw-02v)
loads jobs.Job by id from the queue msg; updates status='queued'→'running'→'finished'|'failed' (aisw-02v)

delivers stdout via aiogram.Bot.send_message (constructor-DI; R-1 mitigation) using tg.output.ChainSplitter standalone for chunking (AD-03: full deliver_output deferred until run-output persistence becomes relevant) — aisw-02v
composes resolve_binary + build_env + neutral_cwd + system_prompt_argv + truncate_stderr for the systemd-run --scope --collect wrapped subprocess (aisw-02v)
parse_recurrence on the schedule-text portion of /cron_add args; escalate → ru usage hint (aisw-02v)
calls create_cron_user_job after parse-success; receives int job_id for the reply (aisw-02v)
resolves user_tz via storage.sessions.users for parse_recurrence and CronTrigger timezone (aisw-02v)
build_router calls register_cron_add_handlers to install the /cron_add Command handler on its Router (aisw-02v)
cron_user.set_cron_user_context(scheduler, queue, jobs_session_maker) after scheduler.start() (aisw-02v)

constructs CronConsumer(queue, bot, claude_binary, claude_config_dir, prompt_path, jobs_session_maker) and spawns asyncio.create_task(consumer.run(), name='aisw.cron_consumer'); cancels on shutdown (aisw-02v)
returns typed outcomes and mutation evidence to the provider-neutral policy
typed Claude limit detection and safe structured fallback selection
gpt-5.4-mini structured output validated by the existing classifier schema
one WIKI lock spans Claude and safe Codex fallback; mutation evidence blocks replay
gpt-5.5 medium JSONL normalized into provider-neutral WIKI events
schema text generation is replay-safe before apply_generated_schema mutates a file
gpt-5.5 medium text fallback for schema generation
cron text execution falls back before Telegram delivery
gpt-5.5 medium text fallback under read-only sandbox
constructs one process-local circuit shared by all Claude-backed paths
runs non-blocking readiness and injects one configured Codex adapter
